533.flavorText: This Pokémon is so muscular and strongly built that even a group of wrestlers could not make it budge an inch.

534.name: Conkeldurr
534.flavorText: It is thought that Conkeldurr taught humans how to make concrete more than 2,000 years ago.

535.name: Tympole
535.flavorText: They warn others of danger by vibrating their cheeks to create a high-pitched sound.

536.name: Palpitoad
536.flavorText: When they vibrate the bumps on their heads, they can make waves in water or earthquake-like vibrations on land.

537.name: Seismitoad
537.flavorText: They shoot paralyzing liquid from their head bumps. They use vibration to hurt their opponents.

538.name: Throh
538.flavorText: When it tightens its belt, it becomes stronger. Wild Throh use vines to weave their own belts.

539.name: Sawk
539.flavorText: The sound of Sawk punching boulders and trees can be heard all the way from the mountains where they train.

540.name: Sewaddle
540.flavorText: Leavanny dress it in clothes they made for it when it hatched. It hides its head in its hood while it is sleeping.

541.name: Swadloon
541.flavorText: Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.

542.name: Leavanny
542.flavorText: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves, using the cutters on its arms and sticky silk.

543.name: Venipede
543.flavorText: Its bite injects a potent poison, enough to paralyze large bird Pokémon that try to prey on it.

544.name: Whirlipede
544.flavorText: Protected by a hard shell, it spins its body like a wheel and crashes furiously into its enemies.

545.name: Scolipede
545.flavorText: With quick movements, it chases down its foes, attacking relentlessly with its horns until it prevails.

546.name: Cottonee
546.flavorText: When attacked, it escapes by shooting cotton from its body. The cotton serves as a decoy to distract the attacker.

547.name: Whimsicott
547.flavorText: Like the wind, it can slip through any gap, no matter how small. It leaves balls of white fluff behind.

548.name: Petilil
548.flavorText: The leaves on its head are very bitter. Eating one of these leaves is known to refresh a tired body.

549.name: Lilligant
549.flavorText: Even veteran Trainers face a challenge in getting its beautiful flower to bloom. This Pokémon is popular with celebrities.

550.name: Basculin-red-striped
550.flavorText: Red and blue Basculin get along so poorly, they’ll start fighting instantly. These Pokémon are very hostile.

551.name: Sandile
551.flavorText: They live buried in the sands of the desert. The sun-warmed sands prevent their body temperature from dropping.

552.name: Krokorok
552.flavorText: They live in groups of a few individuals. Protective membranes shield their eyes from sandstorms.

553.name: Krookodile
553.flavorText: They never allow prey to escape. Their jaws are so powerful, they can crush the body of an automobile.

554.name: Darumaka
554.flavorText: When its internal fire is burning, it cannot calm down and it runs around. When the fire diminishes, it falls asleep.

555.name: Darmanitan-standard
555.flavorText: Its internal fire burns at 2,500° F, making enough power that it can destroy a dump truck with one punch.

556.name: Maractus
556.flavorText: It uses an up-tempo song and dance to drive away the bird Pokémon that prey on its flower seeds.

557.name: Dwebble
557.flavorText: This Pokémon can easily melt holes in hard rocks with a liquid secreted from its mouth.

558.name: Crustle
558.flavorText: Competing for territory, Crustle fight viciously. The one whose boulder is broken is the loser of the battle.

559.name: Scraggy
559.flavorText: Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling its skin up to its neck.

560.name: Scrafty
560.flavorText: Groups of them beat up anything that enters their territory. Each can spit acidic liquid from its mouth.

561.name: Sigilyph
561.flavorText: They never vary the route they fly, because their memories of guarding an ancient city remain steadfast.

562.name: Yamask
562.flavorText: Each of them carries a mask that used to be its face when it was human. Sometimes they look at it and cry.

563.name: Cofagrigus
563.flavorText: It has been said that they swallow those who get too close and turn them into mummies. They like to eat gold nuggets.

564.name: Tirtouga
564.flavorText: Restored from a fossil, this Pokémon can dive to depths beyond half a mile.

565.name: Carracosta
565.flavorText: They can live both in the ocean and on land. A slap from one of them is enough to open a hole in the bottom of a tanker.

566.name: Archen
566.flavorText: Said to be an ancestor of bird Pokémon, they were unable to fly and moved about by hopping from one branch to another.

567.name: Archeops
567.flavorText: They are intelligent and will cooperate to catch prey. From the ground, they use a running start to take flight.

568.name: Trubbish
568.flavorText: Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.

569.name: Garbodor
569.flavorText: It clenches opponents with its left arm and finishes them off with foul-smelling poison gas belched from its mouth.

570.name: Zorua
570.flavorText: It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

571.name: Zoroark
571.flavorText: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.

572.name: Minccino
572.flavorText: They greet one another by rubbing each other with their tails, which are always kept well groomed and clean.

573.name: Cinccino
573.flavorText: Their white fur is coated in a special oil that makes it easy for them to deflect attacks.

574.name: Gothita
574.flavorText: Their ribbonlike feelers increase their psychic power. They are always staring at something.

575.name: Gothorita
575.flavorText: They use hypnosis to control people and Pokémon. Tales of Gothorita leading people astray are told in every corner.

576.name: Gothitelle
576.flavorText: Starry skies thousands of light-years away are visible in the space distorted by their intense psychic power.

577.name: Solosis
577.flavorText: They drive away attackers by unleashing psychic power. They can use telepathy to talk with others.

578.name: Duosion
578.flavorText: Since they have two divided brains, at times they suddenly try to take two different actions at once.

579.name: Reuniclus
579.flavorText: When Reuniclus shake hands, a network forms between their brains, increasing their psychic power.

580.name: Ducklett
580.flavorText: These bird Pokémon are excellent divers. They swim around in the water eating their favorite food--peat moss.

581.name: Swanna
581.flavorText: Swanna start to dance at dusk. The one dancing in the middle is the leader of the flock.

582.name: Vanillite
582.flavorText: The temperature of their breath is -58° F. They create snow crystals and make snow fall in the areas around them.

583.name: Vanillish
583.flavorText: Snowy mountains are this Pokémon’s habitat. During an ancient ice age, they moved to southern areas.

584.name: Vanilluxe
584.flavorText: Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and attack their foes with violent blizzards.

585.name: Deerling
585.flavorText: The color and scent of their fur changes to match the mountain grass. When they sense hostility, they hide in the grass.

586.name: Sawsbuck
586.flavorText: They migrate according to the seasons. People can tell the season by looking at Sawsbuck’s horns.

587.name: Emolga
587.flavorText: The energy made in its cheeks’ electric pouches is stored inside its membrane and released while it is gliding.

588.name: Karrablast
588.flavorText: These mysterious Pokémon evolve when they receive electrical stimulation while they are in the same place as Shelmet.

589.name: Escavalier
589.flavorText: They fly around at high speed, striking with their pointed spears. Even when in trouble, they face opponents bravely.

590.name: Foongus
590.flavorText: It lures people in with its Poké Ball pattern, then releases poison spores. Why it resembles a Poké Ball is unknown.

591.name: Amoonguss
591.flavorText: It lures prey close by dancing and waving its arm caps, which resemble Poké Balls, in a swaying motion.

592.name: Frillish-male
592.flavorText: With its thin, veil-like arms wrapped around the body of its opponent, it sinks to the ocean floor.

593.name: Jellicent-male
593.flavorText: The fate of the ships and crew that wander into Jellicent’s habitat: all sunken, all lost, all vanished.

594.name: Alomomola
594.flavorText: The special membrane enveloping Alomomola has the ability to heal wounds.

595.name: Joltik
595.flavorText: Joltik that live in cities have learned a technique for sucking electricity from the outlets in houses.

596.name: Galvantula
596.flavorText: When attacked, they create an electric barrier by spitting out many electrically charged threads.

597.name: Ferroseed
597.flavorText: When threatened, it attacks by shooting a barrage of spikes, which gives it a chance to escape by rolling away.

598.name: Ferrothorn
598.flavorText: It fights by swinging around its three spiky feelers. A hit from these steel spikes can reduce a boulder to rubble.

599.name: Klink
599.flavorText: The two minigears that mesh together are predetermined. Each will rebound from other minigears without meshing.

600.name: Klang
600.flavorText: By changing the direction in which it rotates, it communicates its feelings to others. When angry, it rotates faster.

601.name: Klinklang
601.flavorText: The gear with the red core is rotated at high speed for a rapid energy charge.

602.name: Tynamo
602.flavorText: While one alone doesn’t have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

603.name: Eelektrik
603.flavorText: They coil around foes and shock them with electricity-generating organs that seem simply to be circular patterns.

604.name: Eelektross
604.flavorText: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

605.name: Elgyem
605.flavorText: It uses its strong psychic power to squeeze its opponent’s brain, causing unendurable headaches.

606.name: Beheeyem
606.flavorText: It can manipulate an opponent’s memory. Apparently, it communicates by flashing its three different-colored fingers.

607.name: Litwick
607.flavorText: Litwick shines a light that absorbs the life energy of people and Pokémon, which becomes the fuel that it burns.

608.name: Lampent
608.flavorText: This ominous Pokémon is feared. Through cities it wanders, searching for the spirits of the fallen.

609.name: Chandelure
609.flavorText: It absorbs a spirit, which it then burns. By waving the flames on its arms, it puts its foes into a hypnotic trance.

610.name: Axew
610.flavorText: They use their tusks to crush the berries they eat. Repeated regrowth makes their tusks strong and sharp.

611.name: Fraxure
611.flavorText: Since a broken tusk will not grow back, they diligently sharpen their tusks on river rocks after they’ve been fighting.

612.name: Haxorus
612.flavorText: They are kind but can be relentless when defending territory. They challenge foes with tusks that can cut steel.

613.name: Cubchoo
613.flavorText: When it is not feeling well, its mucus gets watery and the power of its Ice-type moves decreases.

614.name: Beartic
614.flavorText: It can make its breath freeze at will. Very able in the water, it swims around in northern seas and catches prey.

615.name: Cryogonal
615.flavorText: When its body temperature goes up, it turns into steam and vanishes. When its temperature lowers, it returns to ice.

616.name: Shelmet
616.flavorText: When attacked, it defends itself by closing the lid of its shell. It can spit a sticky, poisonous liquid.

617.name: Accelgor
617.flavorText: When its body dries out, it weakens. So, to prevent dehydration, it wraps itself in many layers of thin membrane.

618.name: Stunfisk
618.flavorText: Its skin is very hard, so it is unhurt even if stepped on by sumo wrestlers. It smiles when transmitting electricity.

619.name: Mienfoo
619.flavorText: In fights, they dominate with onslaughts of flowing, continuous attacks. With their sharp claws, they cut enemies.

620.name: Mienshao
620.flavorText: It wields the fur on its arms like a whip. Its arm attacks come with such rapidity that they cannot even be seen.

621.name: Druddigon
621.flavorText: It warms its body by absorbing sunlight with its wings. When its body temperature falls, it can no longer move.

622.name: Golett
622.flavorText: The energy that burns inside it enables it to move, but no one has yet been able to identify this energy.

623.name: Golurk
623.flavorText: It flies across the sky at Mach speeds. Removing the seal on its chest makes its internal energy go out of control.

624.name: Pawniard
624.flavorText: Blades comprise this Pokémon’s entire body. If battling dulls the blades, it sharpens them on stones by the river.

625.name: Bisharp
625.flavorText: It leads a group of Pawniard. It battles to become the boss, but will be driven from the group if it loses.

626.name: Bouffalant
626.flavorText: Their fluffy fur absorbs damage, even if they strike foes with a fierce headbutt.

627.name: Rufflet
627.flavorText: They crush berries with their talons. They bravely stand up to any opponent, no matter how strong it is.

628.name: Braviary
628.flavorText: They fight for their friends without any thought about danger to themselves. One can carry a car while flying.

629.name: Vullaby
629.flavorText: Their wings are too tiny to allow them to fly. They guard their posteriors with bones that were gathered by Mandibuzz.

630.name: Mandibuzz
630.flavorText: It makes a nest out of bones it finds. It grabs weakened prey in its talons and hauls it to its nest of bones.

631.name: Heatmor
631.flavorText: It breathes through a hole in its tail while it burns with an internal fire. Durant is its prey.

632.name: Durant
632.flavorText: They attack in groups, covering themselves in steel armor to protect themselves from Heatmor.

633.name: Deino
633.flavorText: It tends to bite everything, and it is not a picky eater. Approaching it carelessly is dangerous.

634.name: Zweilous
634.flavorText: After it has eaten up all the food in its territory, it moves to another area. Its two heads do not get along.

635.name: Hydreigon
635.flavorText: This brutal Pokémon travels the skies on its six wings. Anything that moves seems like a foe to it, triggering its attack.

636.name: Larvesta
636.flavorText: This Pokémon was believed to have been born from the sun. When it evolves, its entire body is engulfed in flames.

637.name: Volcarona
637.flavorText: When volcanic ash darkened the atmosphere, it is said that Volcarona’s fire provided a replacement for the sun.

638.name: Cobalion
638.flavorText: This legendary Pokémon battled against humans to protect Pokémon. Its personality is calm and composed.

639.name: Terrakion
639.flavorText: This Pokémon came to the defense of Pokémon that had lost their homes in a war among humans.

640.name: Virizion
640.flavorText: This Pokémon fought humans in order to protect its friends. Legends about it continue to be passed down.

641.name: Tornadus-incarnate
641.flavorText: The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.

642.name: Thundurus-incarnate
642.flavorText: Countless charred remains mar the landscape of places through which Thundurus has passed.

643.name: Reshiram
643.flavorText: This Pokémon appears in legends. It sends flames into the air from its tail, burning up everything around it.

644.name: Zekrom
644.flavorText: Concealing itself in lightning clouds, it flies throughout the Unova region. It creates electricity in its tail.

645.name: Landorus-incarnate
645.flavorText: Lands visited by Landorus grant such bountiful crops that it has been hailed as “The Guardian of the Fields.”

646.name: Kyurem
646.flavorText: It generates a powerful, freezing energy inside itself, but its body became frozen when the energy leaked out.

647.name: Keldeo-ordinary
647.flavorText: By blasting water from its hooves, it can glide across water. It excels at using leg moves while battling.

648.name: Meloetta-aria
648.flavorText: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.

649.name: Genesect
649.flavorText: Over 300 million years ago, it was feared as the strongest of hunters. It has been modified by Team Plasma.
